title: Classical Ciphers and Cryptanalysis as Defect Labs · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent team receives different assets, adversaries, data sizes, cryptoperiods, interoperability duties, availability limits, and compliance claims and transfers classical ciphers and cryptanalysis as defect labs into a new classical cipher attack and migration dossier. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of classical cipher attack and migration dossier with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Implement and break a bounded shift or substitution cipher to connect keyspace, language redundancy, frequency evidence, and attacker cost.
Demonstrate how repeated XOR keystream use reveals relationships between plaintexts without presenting XOR alone as production encryption.
Explain one-time-pad key length, uniformity, secrecy, single use, distribution, and destruction requirements and their operational cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Caesar Frequency Break
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab cryptographic engineering team handles case 9218 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Caesar Frequency Break.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for implement and break a bounded shift or substitution cipher to connect keyspace, language redundancy, frequency evidence, and attacker cost. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Caesar Frequency Break, keep Crypto Go Caesar Frequency Break connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A lab cryptographic engineering team handles case 1887 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Legacy cryptography can remain available indefinitely if marked deprecated.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria. Artifact under review: classical cipher attack and migration dossier.

options.text: The Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary record says classical cipher attack and migration dossier looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the proposed evidence packet evidence boundary for Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary.

For audit the proposed evidence packet, run one deterministic Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary model, omit canonical bytes, asset, adversary, key and nonce ownership, tamper rejection, secret-safe failure, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result cryptographically secure. This does not satisfy Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary for classical cipher attack and migration dossier.
The record names the assumption, applies Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-legacy-migration-boundary-1cie8c" that uses case 2774 to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.

steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab cryptographic engineering team handles case 5125 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.
